Emergency response planning covering medical (Landspitali National University Hospital, medevac to London or Copenhagen), fire, intrusion, and relevant local threats is completed before occupancy begins.

item.title: Reykjavik
item.description: Reykjavik is the most operationally straightforward security environment in this city network. The ambient crime rate is negligible, civil unrest is essentially absent, and the political environment is stable. The operational security discipline for corporate visitors to Iceland is almost entirely directed at environmental risk: volcanic activity on the Reykjanes peninsula is an active and documented concern (IMO 2025/2026), and the 50km KEF to Reykjavik transfer corridor is exposed to severe weather conditions that require active monitoring and contingency routing for executive transport. These are logistics and risk management challenges rather than personal security threats.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Bratislava, Slovakia
item.description: Bratislava, Slovakia’s capital, sits in an unusual dual-airport geography: the city’s own Bratislava Airport M. R. Stefanik (BTS) is just 9 km northeast of the centre, but many visitors, particularly those arriving from outside Central Europe, fly into Vienna International Airport (VIE), approximately 60 km to the northwest, and transfer by road. The UK FCDO advises normal precautions for Slovakia as of 2026, and the US State Department rates the country at Level 1. Slovakia is an EU and NATO member state with a stable political environment and a low violent crime rate.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Ljubljana, Slovenia
item.description: Ljubljana, Slovenia’s compact and walkable capital, is served by Ljubljana Joze Pucnik Airport (LJU), also known as Brnik Airport, approximately 26 km to the north. The UK FCDO advises normal precautions for Slovenia as of 2026, and the US State Department rates the country at Level 1. Slovenia is an EU, NATO, and Schengen member state with a consistently low crime rate and a stable political environment. For most business visitors, the principal transfer considerations are route timing on the A2/A1 motorway corridor and the access conditions within Ljubljana’s historic city centre rather than security threats per se.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Minsk, Belarus
item.description: Minsk, the capital of Belarus, is served by Minsk National Airport (MSQ), approximately 42 km east of the city centre. However, the overriding context for any consideration of a Minsk airport transfer is the UK Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office’s advice against all travel to Belarus as of 2026, and the US State Department’s Level 4 (Do Not Travel) rating for the same period. These are the highest advisory levels issued by both governments. Most major Western airlines do not serve MSQ and do not transit Belarusian airspace, following the 2021 forced diversion of a Ryanair aircraft over Belarus. Commercial operations in Belarus are subject to comprehensive EU, UK, and US sanctions. The Lukashenko government’s documented practice of arbitrary detention of foreign nationals, including business visitors, journalists, and activists, means that any ground movement in Minsk carries materially elevated risk that cannot be mitigated through standard close protection measures alone.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Nicosia, Cyprus
item.description: Nicosia, Cyprus’s capital, is a unique destination in the European context: it remains the world’s last divided capital city, split by the UN buffer zone between the Greek Cypriot south and the Turkish Cypriot north. The UK FCDO advises normal precautions for the Republic of Cyprus as of 2026 and advises against travel to the northern occupied area. The US State Department rates Cyprus at Level 1 as of 2026. Despite the low ambient risk level, the city’s divided character and the absence of a commercial airport in the capital itself introduce logistical considerations that benefit from professional coordination.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Riga, Latvia
item.description: Riga, Latvia’s capital and the largest city in the Baltic states, is served by Riga International Airport (RIX), approximately 10 km southwest of the historic Old Town. The UK FCDO advises normal precautions for Latvia as of 2026, and the US State Department rates the country at Level 1. As an EU and NATO member state, Latvia operates within a stable governance framework, though its geographic position and the post-2022 security environment in the broader Baltic region mean that business visitors in sensitive sectors benefit from a considered approach to ground movements and information security.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Sofia, Bulgaria
item.description: Sofia, Bulgaria’s capital, is served by Sofia Airport (SOF), with Terminal 2 as the main international gateway, approximately 10 km east of the city centre. The UK FCDO advises normal precautions for Bulgaria as of 2026 but specifically notes that organised crime and corruption exist at levels above the EU average. The US State Department rates Bulgaria at Level 1 as of 2026. The ambient risk to visiting foreign nationals is low in terms of physical threat, but the commercial environment requires appropriate due diligence, and the well-documented unlicensed taxi situation at SOF Terminal 2 makes pre-arranged collection a practical necessity rather than a preference.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Tallinn, Estonia
item.description: Tallinn, Estonia’s compact capital, is served by Lennart Meri Tallinn Airport (TLL), just 4 km east of the city centre. The UK FCDO advises normal precautions for Estonia as of 2026, and the US State Department rates the country at Level 1. Estonia’s position as an EU and NATO member state, combined with its role as a global reference point for digital governance and cybersecurity, makes Tallinn a destination for an unusually concentrated mix of government, technology, defence, and financial services visitors. NATO’s Cooperative Cyber Defence Centre of Excellence (CCDCOE) is based in the city, giving the information security dimension of any visit practical relevance.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Valletta, Malta
item.description: Valletta, Malta’s compact capital on the northeastern coast, is served exclusively by Malta International Airport (MLA) in Gudja, approximately 9 km to the south. The UK FCDO advises normal precautions for Malta as of 2026, and the US State Department places the country at Level 1. Despite a benign threat environment, the open concourse arrivals hall at MLA and the presence of informal taxi operators outside the terminal mean that pre-arranged, named collection materially reduces uncertainty for arriving executives and HNW principals.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Vilnius, Lithuania
item.description: Vilnius, Lithuania’s capital, is served by Vilnius Airport (VNO), just 6 km south of the city centre, one of the closest airport-to-capital distances in the EU. The UK FCDO advises normal precautions for Lithuania as of 2026, and the US State Department rates the country at Level 1. Lithuania’s membership of both the EU and NATO places it within a well-established multilateral security framework, though its geographic position, proximity to Belarus, and strategic importance to NATO’s eastern flank mean that business visitors in sensitive sectors benefit from a professional, discrete approach to ground logistics.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Zagreb, Croatia
item.description: Zagreb, Croatia’s capital, is served by Zagreb Airport Franjo Tudjman (ZAG), approximately 17 km southeast of the city centre. The UK FCDO advises normal precautions for Croatia as of 2026, and the US State Department rates the country at Level 1. Croatia joined the Schengen area and the euro zone in January 2023, a milestone that has increased corporate visitor traffic and simplified intra-EU business operations. For most business travellers, the practical transfer considerations centre on route timing on the A3 motorway approach, periodic construction congestion near the airport, and access conditions within Zagreb’s historic Upper Town district.

item.title: Security Drivers in Athens, Greece
item.description: Athens combines a manageable overall risk environment with some of Europe’s most demanding urban driving conditions: severe congestion, frequent protest closures of central arterials, and narrow historic-district streets that eliminate many routing options. The 33km distance from ATH to the city centre via the Attiki Odos means journey time is heavily influenced by driver route knowledge and real-time traffic monitoring. Our Athens city overview provides detailed context on protest patterns, neighbourhood-level risk factors, and emergency infrastructure.

item.title: Security Drivers in Barcelona, Spain
item.description:
Security driver services in Barcelona are structured around the medium risk environment and the specific operational requirements of Eixample and Diagonal business corridor; Sarrià-Sant Gervasi for residential and the BCN airport transfer corridor. FCDO advises normal precautions with heightened terrorism awareness, Spain (2026); US State Dept Level 2 (Increased Caution - terrorism, crime), Spain (2026).

All vetted security drivers for Barcelona operations comply with Ley de Seguridad Privada 5/2014; DGSN licence required and are multi-point vetted for identity, criminal history, driving competence, and route knowledge. For additional security services, see our bodyguard hire in Barcelona and our Barcelona city security briefing. For regional transport coverage, see our security drivers in Madrid.

item.title: Security Drivers in Belgrade, Serbia
item.description:
Belgrade’s urban road environment, with limited Sava bridge crossings and variable outer-district road quality, makes professional driver knowledge of the city’s routing options a genuine operational asset. Scam taxi risk at Nikola Tesla Airport (BEG) makes pre-arranged collection the standard practice for principals. Our Belgrade city overview provides wider threat context, neighbourhood-level situational awareness guidance, and emergency infrastructure details for visit planning.

For principals requiring integrated transport and close protection, our executive protection services in Belgrade provide CP teams, advance work, and vetted transport under a coordinated deployment plan. Both services are delivered by operators holding current MUP licensing under Serbia’s Law on Private Security (Official Gazette 104/2013).

item.description:

item.title: Security Drivers in Bucharest, Romania
item.description:
Bucharest requires a security driving approach that treats road risk as the primary variable rather than threat from third parties. Romania’s road fatality rate, among the highest in the EU according to Eurostat 2024, makes driver selection and vehicle maintenance as important as any other protective measure. Our Bucharest city overview covers the broader security context, including petty crime patterns and areas requiring heightened situational awareness in the capital.

For principals requiring integrated close protection and secure transport, our executive protection services in Bucharest provide a coordinated team covering advance work, principal briefing, and continuous protective posture throughout the visit. Both services are delivered by operators holding current Ministry of Interior licensing under Law 333/2003, with ANSSI-registered companies available on request.

item.title: Security Drivers in Budapest, Hungary
item.description:
Budapest’s divided geography, with Buda and Pest separated by the Danube and connected by a small number of bridge crossings, makes route planning a meaningful operational variable rather than a formality. Peak-hour bridge congestion can materially affect journey times between the airport and Buda-side destinations such as the Castle District. Our Budapest city overview provides detailed context on local risk factors, bridge crossing dynamics, and emergency infrastructure across both banks.

For principals requiring integrated transport and close protection, our executive protection services in Budapest coordinate CP teams, advance work, and secure transport under a single deployment plan. Both services are delivered by operators holding current ORFK licensing under Act CXXXIII of 2005.

item.title: Security Drivers in Copenhagen, Denmark
item.description: Copenhagen is one of Europe’s easiest capitals for executive transport: a compact city, an 8km airport-to-centre distance (the shortest of any major European capital), excellent road infrastructure, and very low ambient crime. The one locally-specific competency required is familiarity with Copenhagen’s extensive cycling infrastructure, where segregated but busy bicycle lanes require consistent urban awareness. Our Copenhagen city overview provides broader context on the security environment, the Oresund region, and emergency infrastructure.

item.title: Security Drivers in Dublin, Ireland
item.description: Dublin’s security driving environment is straightforward in threat terms but demanding in traffic management: the M50 orbital and Port Tunnel are subject to severe congestion, and the 10km DUB-to-city distance can translate to 40-minute journeys at peak hours without a driver who knows the alternative routing via Drumcondra Road, Strand Road, and the R131. Terminal confirmation at Dublin Airport (T1 or T2) is also an operational detail that matters. Our Dublin city overview provides detailed context on the city’s road network, security environment, and emergency infrastructure.

item.title: Security Drivers in Helsinki, Finland
item.description:
Helsinki’s primary security driving consideration is not threat from third parties but winter weather competence: ice and snow from October to March create specific risks on Ring Road III and the E18 corridor that a driver without Finnish winter driving experience handles materially less well than a local expert. Our Helsinki city overview provides broader context on the city’s security environment, emergency infrastructure, and operational considerations for business visits.

For principals requiring integrated transport and close protection, our executive protection services in Helsinki coordinate CP teams, advance work, and vetted transport under a single deployment plan. item.title: Stockholm

The security environment is low-risk for standard corporate itineraries: FCDO issues normal precautions and the US State Dept maintains a Level 1 advisory for Sweden in 2026. The terrorism awareness context, elevated since 2023 by the Swedish Security Service (SAPO) following threats related to Quran burning incidents, is a relevant planning factor for high-profile public-facing assignments. item.title: Event Security in Yerevan, Armenia
item.description: Yerevan’s evolving geopolitical position, as Armenia deepens EU engagement while managing complex relationships with Russia, Iran and Azerbaijan, has made the Armenian capital an increasingly significant venue for diplomatic, energy and technology conferences. The FCDO Armenia travel advice (2026) advises a high degree of caution, and the post-2023 regional environment means that event security planning for internationally attended gatherings must incorporate demonstration monitoring, geopolitical intelligence, secure transport and a tested emergency response protocol. For the complete Yerevan security environment and city-level risk assessment, see our Yerevan city security overview.

item.title: Event Security in Zagreb, Croatia
item.description:
Event security in Zagreb addresses the specific operational requirements of internationally attended conferences and summits in Croatia’s low risk environment. Normal precautions for croatia (2026); US State Dept Level 1 (Exercise Normal Precautions) for Croatia (2026).

Effective event security planning for Zagreb begins with an intelligence review covering the current local political situation, known protest scheduling, and active Croatia advisories. Venue access management at Esplanade Zagreb Hotel (Mihanoviceva 1) and other principal conference properties requires credential verification, zone segregation, and specific arrival protocols for senior delegates. Secure transport from Franjo Tudman International Airport (ZAG) and medevac pre-arrangement to Vienna or Ljubljana are standard components of the pre-event plan.

item.title: Executive Protection in Bratislava, Slovakia
item.description: Executive protection in Bratislava is a programme, not a single officer. It is the discipline a company applies to protect a travelling executive against background-level organised crime and political-environment exposure: advance work on venues and routes, a written risk assessment, an operations controller, and Ministry of the Interior licensed secure transport integrated with the corporate itinerary. This page is for security managers designing protection around a C-suite visit, where the need is layered, documented risk management.

This page is for security managers designing protection around a C-suite visit, where the need is layered, documented risk management rather than ad-hoc cover.

item.title: Executive Protection in Minsk, Belarus
item.description: Executive protection in Minsk is a programme built for one of Europe’s most demanding operating environments, not a single officer. It is the discipline a company applies when essential travel cannot be avoided despite arbitrary-detention risk and comprehensive sanctions: continuous surveillance-aware planning, an operations controller, a ground-medevac relationship toward Vilnius, and current-Belarus-experienced secure transport integrated into the itinerary. This page is for security managers designing protection around a visit, where the need is layered, documented risk management.

item.title: Ljubljana
item.description: Ljubljana is one of the most straightforward operational environments for corporate security in Central Europe. Slovenia’s small size, stable governance, and EU and NATO membership produce a capital city with very low rates of crime, minimal political instability, and a professional emergency services infrastructure. The city centre is compact and walkable, with the Ljubljanica River and castle providing a distinctive but easily navigated geography. The Slovenian Police maintain a consistent presence in the city centre and response times are fast.

item.title: Minsk
item.description: Minsk is one of the most operationally constrained European capitals for foreign nationals. The Lukashenko government’s violent suppression of the 2020 post-election protests, the forced diversion of a Ryanair flight to arrest a journalist in 2021, and Belarus’s facilitation of Russia’s 2022 invasion of Ukraine have resulted in FCDO advising against all but essential travel and US State Department Level 4 (Do Not Travel). For the overwhelming majority of corporate visitors, these advisories mean travel to Minsk is not appropriate. The combination of arbitrary detention risk, comprehensive EU, UK, and US sanctions, and pervasive communications surveillance creates an operating environment that requires specialist pre-deployment planning for any essential-travel mission.

item.title: Nicosia
item.description: Nicosia’s status as Europe’s only divided capital gives it an unusual operational context that is distinct from its generally low threat environment. The Republic of Cyprus side of the city functions as a normal, modern EU capital with well-developed infrastructure, a professional police service, and low rates of serious crime. The division matters for route planning and accommodation selection: the historic old city near the Green Line is a navigational constraint, and principals should be briefed not to approach the UN Buffer Zone boundary outside designated crossings. UNFICYP has maintained the ceasefire since 1974 and the buffer zone itself is stable, but its boundaries are not universally signposted and unintentional proximity can attract police attention.

item.title: Residential Security in Bratislava, Slovakia
item.description:
Residential security in Bratislava addresses the requirements of expatriate executives, diplomatic staff, and high-net-worth individuals requiring protected accommodation in Slovakia’s low risk environment. Normal precautions for slovakia (2026); US State Dept Level 1 (Exercise Normal Precautions) for Slovakia (2026).

A professional residential security deployment for Bratislava begins with a property security survey covering perimeter, access control, natural surveillance, lighting, and neighbourhood risk. Guard-force selection follows, with all personnel vetted to the standards of the Bureau of Security Service Oversight (USOO), Ministry of Interior. Emergency response planning – covering medical emergency (nearest facility University Hospital Bratislava (FNsP Bratislava, medevac to Vienna or Prague), fire, intrusion, and civil disturbance – is completed before occupancy begins.

item.title: Residential Security in Minsk, Belarus
item.description:
Residential security in Minsk addresses the requirements of expatriate executives, diplomatic staff, and high-net-worth individuals requiring protected accommodation in Belarus’s high risk environment. Advises against all but essential travel to belarus (2026); arbitrary detention risk is explicitly cited; US State Dept Level 4 (Do Not Travel) for Belarus (2026).

A professional residential security deployment for Minsk begins with a property security survey covering perimeter, access control, natural surveillance, lighting, and neighbourhood risk. Guard-force selection follows, with all personnel vetted to the standards of the Ministry of Interior of Belarus. Emergency response planning – covering medical emergency (nearest facility Minsk 9th Clinical Hospital (public); private options are limited; medevac strongly recommended for serious cases, medevac to Vilnius (Lithuania) or Warsaw (Poland)), fire, intrusion, and civil disturbance – is completed before occupancy begins.

item.title: Riga
item.description: Riga is a well-functioning EU and NATO capital with a low physical threat environment for corporate visitors. The city’s Art Nouveau architecture, compact city centre, and established conference infrastructure make it a practical destination for Baltic and Northern European business travel. Standard personal security awareness - particularly regarding pickpocketing in tourist areas of the Old Town and Central Market - is the primary operational consideration for most visitors. The Riga State Police maintains visible uniformed patrols in the city centre and response times are consistent with Western European norms.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Chisinau, Moldova
item.description: Chisinau International Airport (KIV) is 13km from the capital’s city centre and handles increasing volumes of EU-linked diplomatic and business travel following Moldova’s EU candidate status. The FCDO advises a high degree of caution in Moldova (2026), partly reflecting the Transnistria situation, and the US State Department maintains a Level 2 designation (2026). Standard airport transfers in Chisinau operate entirely within the capital area and are not affected by the Transnistria restriction. Informal taxi operators are present in the external vehicle area at KIV, and pre-arranged collection inside the arrivals hall is the appropriate standard for diplomatic and corporate principals. Our team positions ahead of each flight and coordinates the 13km transfer to the city centre using a vetted, locally licensed driver.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Cotonou, Benin
item.description: Cadjehoun Airport (COO) sits close to the centre of Cotonou, Benin’s commercial capital, and handles a significant volume of trade, port, and energy sector arrivals. The immediate airport environment is characterised by heavy zemidjan motorcycle taxi activity outside the arrivals hall, creating a disorganised and security-sensitive exit area that is not appropriate for unaccompanied corporate principals. Our inside-terminal collection protocol ensures principals are met before they reach that environment, with a vetted vehicle and briefed driver ready for immediate departure. The FCDO advises a high degree of caution in Benin (2026), and the US State Department maintains a Level 2 designation (2026).

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Lome, Togo
item.description: Lome-Tokoin International Airport (LFW) sits approximately 6km from central Lome and handles a diverse mix of trade, investment, and West African regional traffic. The FCDO advises a high degree of caution throughout Togo (2026), and the US State Department maintains a Level 2 designation (2026). Unlicensed taxi operators are persistent in the arrivals area, making pre-arranged vetted collection inside the terminal the correct baseline for any corporate or high-profile principal. Our team arrives ahead of each flight, tracks status in real time, and positions to meet principals before they reach the external vehicle rank.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Malabo, Equatorial Guinea
item.description: Malabo International Airport (SSG) serves Bioko Island as the primary international gateway for Equatorial Guinea’s oil industry, diplomatic community, and government-linked travel. The operating environment is classified as high-risk by the FCDO (2026), and informal transport at the airport presents a direct threat to corporate and diplomatic arrivals who are not met by a pre-arranged, vetted vehicle. Our team positions inside the arrivals hall ahead of each flight, coordinates customs and immigration assistance where permitted, and moves principals directly to a Ministry of National Security-licensed vehicle without exposure to unsolicited drivers or waiting areas.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Podgorica, Montenegro
item.description: Podgorica Airport (TGD) is 12km south of Montenegro’s capital and serves a growing mix of investment, diplomatic, and high-profile leisure arrivals drawn by Montenegro’s NATO membership and Adriatic coastal reputation. The FCDO advises a high degree of caution in Montenegro (2026), with a specific warning about bystander risk from Kavac-Skaljari organised crime violence, and the US State Department maintains a Level 2 designation (2026). This targeted violence context means that even routine corporate transfers warrant low-profile vehicles, route variation, and minimised dwell time in open areas. Our team meets principals at TGD, positions the vehicle appropriately, and moves through the 12km corridor to the city centre using an unmarked, vetted vehicle operated by a locally licensed driver.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Praia, Cape Verde
item.description: Nelson Mandela International Airport (RAI) is a compact, well-organised facility located 3km from central Praia. Cape Verde benefits from one of the most stable security environments in West Africa, with the FCDO advising standard precautions (2026) and the US State Department maintaining a Level 1 designation (2026). For most visitors, the risk level does not demand elevated transfer protocols. However, HNW individuals, high-profile executives, and principals with sensitive itineraries routinely choose pre-arranged vetted transport as a matter of good practice rather than necessity, and Praia is no exception to that principle.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Sarajevo, Bosnia and Herzegovina
item.description: Sarajevo International Airport (SJJ) is 6km from the Bosnian capital and is operated by Vienna Airport Management, maintaining good facilities for a city of Sarajevo’s size. The airport handles growing diplomatic and investment event traffic as Bosnia and Herzegovina advances its EU integration process. The FCDO advises a high degree of caution in BiH (2026), partly reflecting the country’s political complexity and rural landmine risk, and the US State Department maintains a Level 2 designation (2026). The transfer corridor between SJJ and central Sarajevo is not affected by the landmine risk, which is confined to rural and mountainous areas, and standard corporate transfer protocols apply within the city. Our team meets principals at SJJ, coordinates the 6km transfer to the city centre, and briefs on the relevant in-country context as part of the handover.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Skopje, North Macedonia
item.description: Skopje Alexander the Great Airport (SKP) is 24km from North Macedonia’s capital and handles a growing volume of corporate, diplomatic, and investment traffic tied to the country’s NATO membership and EU accession candidacy. The FCDO advises a high degree of caution for the Balkans region (2026), and the US State Department rates North Macedonia Level 2 (2026). Skopje itself is a stable, functional capital with a standard corporate operating environment, and standard transfer protocols apply on the 24km corridor between SKP and the city centre. Unlicensed taxi operators are present at the airport, and pre-arranged collection avoids any exposure to unsolicited vehicles. Our team meets principals at SKP and coordinates the transfer to central Skopje using a vetted, locally licensed driver in an appropriate vehicle.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Tirana, Albania
item.description: Tirana International Airport Mother Teresa (TIA) lies 17km from the Albanian capital and has seen a steady increase in corporate, diplomatic, and NGO traffic as Albania advances its EU accession process. The FCDO advises a high degree of caution in Albania (2026), and the US State Department maintains a Level 2 designation (2026). Unlicensed taxi operators are a documented presence outside the TIA arrivals exit, and pre-arranged collection inside the terminal is the appropriate standard for all corporate and diplomatic principals. Our team positions before each flight’s expected exit and meets principals inside the arrivals hall, proceeding directly to a vetted, locally licensed vehicle for the 17km transfer to central Tirana.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Yerevan, Armenia
item.description: Zvartnots International Airport (EVN) sits 12km west of central Yerevan and handles a growing volume of European business connections and regional diplomatic traffic. The FCDO advises a high degree of caution in Armenia (2026), reflecting the regional security context, and the US State Department maintains a Level 2 designation (2026). Informal taxi operators are present in the external vehicle area at Zvartnots, and pre-arranged vetted collection inside the arrivals hall is the appropriate standard for corporate and diplomatic principals. Our team positions ahead of each flight, tracks arrival in real time, and coordinates the 12km transfer into central Yerevan using a vetted, locally licensed driver in an appropriate vehicle.

item.title: Security Drivers in Bratislava, Slovakia
item.description:
Security driver services in Bratislava are structured around the low risk environment and the specific operational requirements of Bratislava’s road network, airport, and principal movement corridors. Normal precautions for slovakia (2026); US State Dept Level 1 (Exercise Normal Precautions) for Slovakia (2026).

All vetted security drivers for Bratislava operations are licensed under the Act on Private Security (Act No. 473/2005 Coll.) and multi-point vetted for identity, criminal history, driving competence, and route knowledge of Bratislava. Airport transfers from Milan Rastislav Stefanik Airport (BTS) use pre-positioned vehicles with routes pre-surveyed to any destination. No app-based or informal transport is appropriate for principal movements in Bratislava.

item.title: Sofia
item.description: Sofia presents a meaningfully different security environment from other EU capitals in this region. FCDO Bulgaria travel advice (2026) advises a high degree of caution - the only EU capital in this briefing series to receive that classification - primarily due to the organised crime operating environment documented by Europol and the Bulgarian Ministry of Interior. For corporate visitors, the primary risk is not violent street crime but the pervasive integrity and due diligence challenge: Bulgaria’s historically low Transparency International CPI score (2024) reflects a commercial environment where corruption risk, counterparty integrity issues, and unofficial facilitation pressures are more likely to be encountered than in Western or Northern European capitals.

item.title: Tallinn
item.description: Tallinn combines the physical security profile of a safe Northern European capital with a digital and information security environment that demands above-average professional awareness from corporate visitors. The Old Town - a UNESCO World Heritage site - is one of the best-preserved medieval city centres in Europe and presents a highly walkable, well-policed environment for business travel. Estonian Police and Border Guard Board statistics (2025) confirm low rates of violent and property crime across the city centre. For standard executive visits, the operational security considerations are primarily informational rather than physical.

item.title: Valletta
item.description: Valletta is one of the smallest and most manageable EU capitals for security operations. As a UNESCO World Heritage city with a compact, walkable historic core, it presents minimal personal security risk for corporate and HNWI visitors. The Malta Police Force maintains a visible uniformed presence in the city centre year-round, and CCTV coverage across the capital is comprehensive. The primary security discipline for visiting executives is information and reputational security rather than physical protection: Valletta’s financial services cluster - anchored by the Malta Financial Services Authority and the iGaming regulatory framework - attracts a concentrated population of high-value principals, and social engineering and fraud risks are proportionate to that density.

item.title: Vilnius
item.description: Vilnius is a compact, high-functioning EU and NATO capital with a low physical threat environment for executive visitors. Lithuania’s economy has expanded significantly as a fintech and shared services hub, attracting international corporate delegations to the city year-round. The Old Town is pedestrian-friendly, well-policed, and has a strong hospitality infrastructure. Personal security risk for standard corporate itineraries in Vilnius is low, and the Lithuanian Police maintain a professional and responsive service.

item.title: Zagreb
item.description: Zagreb entered the Schengen Area in January 2023, completing Croatia’s integration into the EU’s free-movement zone and simplifying cross-border executive travel across Central Europe and the Adriatic region. The city has invested significantly in business and conference infrastructure, positioning itself as a MICE destination between Vienna and the Western Balkans. Personal security risk for corporate visitors is low: Croatian Police maintain a visible presence in the city centre, and violent crime rates are well below the EU average.

item.title: Bodyguard Hire in Chisinau, Moldova
item.description: Hiring a bodyguard in Chisinau is usually tied to a specific visit, where the Transnistria frozen conflict and Russian hybrid-influence risk shape the plan alongside organised crime. This page covers how close protection hire works in Moldova, what a Ministry of Internal Affairs licensed officer under Law No. 283/2006 does across a day of movements, and how quickly cover is arranged. Armed cover requires specific authorisation, so hire pairs a licensed officer with secure transport, scaled to the itinerary.

item.title: Bodyguard Hire in Cotonou, Benin
item.description: Hiring a bodyguard in Cotonou is usually tied to a specific visit, where terrorism spillover from the north and Gulf of Guinea maritime risk shape the plan alongside political tension and demonstrations. This page covers how close protection hire works in Benin, what a Ministry of Interior licensed operator under Decree No. 2005-344 does across a day of movements, and how quickly cover is arranged. Deployments typically use unarmed guards with armed support for the highest-risk principals.

item.title: Chisinau
item.description: Chisinau is the capital of Moldova, one of Europe’s smallest and most economically fragile states, positioned between Ukraine to the north and east and Romania to the west. The country’s security environment is defined above all by the Transnistria frozen conflict – the unresolved 1992 civil war that left the eastern strip of Moldova under de facto Russian-supported separatist control – and by the broader implications of Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine for Moldova’s geopolitical exposure.

item.title: Close Protection Officers in Antananarivo
item.description:
This page is about the people who provide close protection in Antananarivo: who they are, how they are vetted, and what credentials to verify before they deploy. Every operator in our Antananarivo network holds national registration, and is assessed for local competence. It covers standard team configuration, the restriction on foreign nationals carrying weapons, and the credential checks a buyer should insist on before engaging any officer.

The CPO environment in Antananarivo

Madagascar’s security environment is shaped by recurrent political instability, an elevated petty crime and vehicle-targeting risk profile, and the specific vulnerabilities of the mining sector in a country with remote and difficult terrain. FCDO advises a high degree of caution in Madagascar, reflecting the combination of crime risk in Antananarivo and road safety concerns across the country. The US State Department rates Madagascar at Level 2 (Exercise Increased Caution). The international zone around Isoraka, Ivandry, and Ankorondrano is materially more stable than the outer districts, but the TNR transfer corridor and night movement remain the primary operational risk windows.

item.title: Close Protection Officers in Freetown
item.description: This page is about the people who provide close protection in Freetown: who they are, how they are vetted, and what credentials to verify before they deploy. Every operator in our Freetown network holds documented registration under Sierra Leone’s Private Security Industry Act, and is assessed for local competence. It covers standard team configuration, the restriction on foreign nationals carrying weapons, and the credential checks a buyer should insist on before engaging any officer.

item.title: Close Protection Officers in Maseru
item.description:
This page is about the people who provide close protection in Maseru: who they are, how they are vetted, and what credentials to verify before they deploy. Every operator in our Maseru network holds appropriate national licensing, and is assessed for local competence. It covers standard team configuration, coordination with the Lesotho Mounted Police, and the credential checks a buyer should insist on before engaging any officer.

The CPO environment in Maseru

Lesotho’s security environment is shaped by periodic political instability, an elevated robbery and vehicle crime risk in Maseru’s outer districts, and the specific operational challenges of the Maluti Mountains for Letseng mine site visits. FCDO advises a heightened degree of caution in Lesotho. The US State Department rates Lesotho at Level 2 (Exercise Increased Caution). The diamond sector (Gem Diamonds Letseng), development finance community (World Bank, AfDB, LHWP bilateral donors), and textile sector form the primary international visitor categories. Source: FCDO Lesotho travel advice, 2026; US State Dept Lesotho Level 2 advisory, 2026.

item.title: Close Protection Officers in Mbabane
item.description: This page is about the people who provide close protection in Mbabane: who they are, how they are vetted, and what credentials to verify before they deploy. Every operator in our Mbabane network holds licensing under Eswatini’s national security legislation, and is assessed for local competence. It covers standard team configuration, the restriction on foreign nationals carrying weapons, and the credential checks a buyer should insist on before engaging any officer.

item.title: Close Protection Officers in Monrovia
item.description: This page is about the people who provide close protection in Monrovia: who they are, how they are vetted, and what credentials to verify before they deploy. It covers standard team configuration, the specific authorisation required for the armed option, and the credential checks a buyer should insist on before engaging any officer.

item.title: Close Protection Officers in Ouagadougou
item.description:
There is no routine CPO deployment offered in Ouagadougou under current conditions: the formal licensing framework is functioning in a degraded form, and the FCDO/US State Department rate Burkina Faso at the highest risk category. This page covers what specialist Sahel conflict-environment operators actually provide, why conventional CPO placement is not offered, and the extreme due-diligence standard applied to any case-by-case essential-purpose engagement.

The CPO environment in Ouagadougou

Burkina Faso’s security environment deteriorated sharply from 2019 onwards following the expansion of Sahel jihadist groups from Mali. The 2022 military coups accelerated the collapse of civilian security structures and the displacement of international security partners. The MPSR junta under Captain Ibrahim Traore has expelled French forces and sought Wagner Group (now Africa Corps) support, fundamentally altering the security architecture. JNIM and IS Sahel Province have conducted complex attacks in Ouagadougou’s commercial areas and hotels used by international visitors. CPO deployment in this environment requires specialist Sahel expertise, current intelligence, and pre-confirmed extraction planning – not standard corporate close protection. Source: FCDO Burkina Faso travel advice, 2026; US State Dept Burkina Faso Level 4 advisory, 2026.

Every operator in our Port Louis network holds PSIA licensing, and is assessed for local competence. It covers standard team configuration, the restriction on foreign nationals carrying weapons, and the credential checks a buyer should insist on before engaging any officer.

item.title: Cotonou
item.description: Cotonou is the largest city and de facto commercial capital of Benin, functioning as the country’s main port city and economic hub despite Porto-Novo holding the formal capital status. The Port of Cotonou handles significant volumes of transit cargo for landlocked neighbours including Niger, Burkina Faso, and Mali. Benin has maintained relative stability in Cotonou and the south, but the northern departments bordering Burkina Faso and Niger have experienced significant jihadist spillover since 2019, placing Benin among the countries at risk of broader Sahel insurgency penetration.

item.title: Event Security in Antananarivo
item.description: Antananarivo is the base for all international regulatory engagement and event activity related to Madagascar’s mining, development, and trade sectors. The FCDO advises a high degree of caution throughout Madagascar and the 14km Ivato airport transfer is the primary security challenge for international delegate management. Registered security operators, inside-terminal collection, Isoraka district venue access management, and pre-arranged medevac to Reunion are the required event security elements. Source: FCDO Madagascar travel advice (2026); US State Dept Madagascar Level 2 advisory (2026).

item.title: Event Security in Freetown
item.description: Freetown event security is defined by the Lungi airport estuary crossing as the dominant logistical challenge, Private Security Industry Act-licensed operators, Aberdeen peninsula venue management, and pre-positioned medevac to Accra or Abidjan. The high-risk environment – documented armed robbery, post-election unrest, and severely limited medical infrastructure – requires a comprehensive event security plan that goes well beyond standard access management. Source: FCDO Sierra Leone travel advice (2026); US State Dept Sierra Leone Level 2 advisory (2026).

item.title: Event Security in Gaborone
item.description:
Gaborone event security is defined by its diamond sector, SADC governance, and development finance event calendar. Private Security Industry Regulation Act-licensed operators, diamond sector information security, SADC protocol coordination, and the straightforward GBE airport transfer are the key elements. Botswana’s low-risk environment and proximity to South Africa make Gaborone one of the more manageable African capitals for international event security planning. Source: FCDO Botswana travel advice (2026).

For the full Gaborone security context, see our Gaborone city briefing. Delegates requiring personal close protection at Gaborone events should review close protection officers in Gaborone.

item.title: Event Security in Maseru
item.description: Maseru event security combines the medium-risk operating environment of Lesotho – armed robbery risk requiring structured access management at the Avani Hotel and Maseru Bridge transfer – with the specific event calendar of the diamond sector, Lesotho Highlands Water Project, and development finance sectors. LMPS-licensed operators, road delegate management from Johannesburg, and South Africa medevac routing define the event security planning framework. Source: FCDO Lesotho travel advice (2026); US State Dept Lesotho Level 2 advisory (2026).

item.title: Event Security in Mbabane
item.description:
Mbabane and Ezulwini Valley event security is shaped by Eswatini’s agribusiness and manufacturing investment event calendar, the absolute monarchy governance context requiring specific protocol awareness, and road delegate management from Johannesburg as the primary international arrival route. Licensed operators with Eswatini-specific credentials, Ezulwini Valley venue management, and South Africa medevac routing define the event security framework. Source: FCDO Eswatini travel advice (2026).

For the full Mbabane security context, see our Mbabane city briefing. Delegates requiring personal close protection at Mbabane events should review close protection officers in Mbabane.

item.title: Event Security in Monrovia
item.description:
Monrovia event security is defined by the 60km Roberts International Airport transfer as the dominant logistical challenge, LNP-licensed operators with post-conflict operating experience, Mamba Point venue management, and pre-positioned medevac to Accra or Abidjan. The high-risk environment and limited police backstop require event security that is designed for self-contained incident response within the venue perimeter. Source: FCDO Liberia travel advice (2026); US State Dept Liberia Level 2 advisory (2026).

For the full Monrovia security context, see our Monrovia city briefing. Delegates requiring personal close protection at Monrovia events should review close protection officers in Monrovia.

item.title: Event Security in Ouagadougou
item.description: Ouagadougou is not a viable venue for internationally attended events under the current extreme risk conditions. JNIM has specifically targeted international locations in the city, and the FCDO advises against all travel to Burkina Faso. This page serves organisations managing Burkina Faso programme meetings from Abidjan or Accra, and those assessing essential mission options. Source: FCDO Burkina Faso travel advice (2026); US State Dept Burkina Faso Level 4 advisory (2026).

item.title: Event Security in Port Louis
item.description: Port Louis’s event security environment is defined by its role as an African financial centre, with investment fund meetings, limited partner conferences, and SIBA compliance events as the primary international event types. PSIA-licensed operators, information security for sensitive financial discussions, HNWI profile management, and the 50km MRU airport transfer logistics are the key event security planning elements. The low-risk operating environment means physical threat mitigation is proportionately modest. Source: FCDO Mauritius travel advice (2026); PSIA Mauritius.

item.title: Event Security in Victoria, Seychelles
item.description:
Victoria event security is calibrated for the Indian Ocean island context: licensed operators on a small island network, HNWI profile management for financial and private gatherings, SEZ airport transfer coordination, and outer island logistics management for charter and speedboat components. The low-risk operating environment means physical threat mitigation is proportionate, with the emphasis on privacy management and professional service delivery. Source: FCDO Seychelles travel advice (2026).

For the full Victoria security context, see our Victoria, Seychelles city briefing. Delegates requiring personal close protection at Victoria events should review close protection officers in Victoria.

item.title: Event Security in Windhoek
item.description:
Windhoek event security is shaped by Namibia’s extractive sector – diamonds, uranium, and emerging critical minerals – combined with the 42km Hosea Kutako airport transfer as the primary logistics consideration for delegate management. Security Officers’ Act-licensed operators, information security for uranium and diamond sector events, and a low-risk operating environment with reliable South African medevac access define the Windhoek event security profile. Source: FCDO Namibia travel advice (2026).

For the full Windhoek security context, see our Windhoek city briefing. Delegates requiring personal close protection at Windhoek events should review close protection officers in Windhoek.

item.title: Executive Protection in Antananarivo
item.description: Executive protection in Antananarivo is a programme, not a single officer. It is the discipline a company applies when it owes a duty of care to an executive facing violent street crime, carjacking, and political instability: advance work on venues and routes, a written threat assessment, an operations controller, and licensed secure transport integrated with the corporate itinerary. This page is for security managers designing protection around a C-suite visit, where the need is layered, documented risk management.

item.title: Executive Protection in Ouagadougou
item.description:
Executive protection in Ouagadougou is not a deployable programme in the conventional sense; the highest FCDO and US State Department risk rating, active insurgency, and mass displacement rule out standard commercial deployment. This page covers what a duty-of-care approach actually requires: specialist Sahel conflict-environment operators, a documented decision framework for whether any Burkina Faso-linked travel proceeds at all, and case-by-case assessment for essential purposes only, not a standard itinerary.

For regional staging and operational management of Burkina Faso interests, Abidjan in Cote d’Ivoire is the primary hub. Accra provides the alternative regional base with direct OUA connections.

item.title: Lome
item.description: Lome is the capital and largest city of Togo, a narrow West African coastal state that serves as a significant regional port hub. The Port of Lome is one of West Africa’s largest deepwater container ports and handles transit goods for several landlocked neighbours including Burkina Faso, Mali, and Niger. The city’s security environment reflects the political durability of Faure Gnassingbe’s government – in power since 2005, following his father Gnassingbe Eyadema who ruled from 1967 – and the broader West African security trajectory that includes Sahel jihadist spillover affecting Togo’s north.

item.title: Malabo
item.description: Malabo is the capital of Equatorial Guinea, a small Central African nation whose oil discoveries in the 1990s transformed it from one of Africa’s poorest economies into its third-largest oil producer at peak production. The city sits on Bioko Island in the Gulf of Guinea, approximately 35 kilometres from the Cameroonian coast. Malabo’s security environment is shaped by the prolonged governance of President Teodoro Obiang Nguema Mbasogo, who has held power since 1979, and by the regulatory and compliance complexities that accompany operating in a state ranked consistently in the lowest decile of Transparency International’s Corruption Perceptions Index.

item.title: Podgorica
item.description: Podgorica is the capital of Montenegro, a small Adriatic nation of approximately 620,000 people that joined NATO in 2017 and has been in EU accession negotiations since 2012. The city’s security environment is dominated by the ongoing conflict between the Kavac and Skaljari organised crime clans – rival drug trafficking networks that have been at war since 2016 and whose conflict has produced targeted killings in public spaces, including incidents in Podgorica itself. The FCDO specifically advises visitors to be alert to the risk of bystander exposure to criminal violence (FCDO Montenegro travel advice, 2026).

item.title: Praia
item.description: Praia is the capital of Cape Verde, an Atlantic archipelago nation approximately 570 kilometres off the West African coast that has established one of Africa’s most stable democratic governance records since independence from Portugal in 1975. Cape Verde is a founding member of the Community of Portuguese Language Countries and maintains close ties with Portugal, Brazil, and the United States, where a significant diaspora community lives. The city’s security environment is among the most benign in the broader region – the FCDO advises standard precautions (FCDO Cape Verde, 2026) and the US State Department advises only normal precautions (Level 1, 2026).

item.title: Residential Security in Antananarivo
item.description:
Residential security in Antananarivo is structured around registered Malagasy operators, the Isoraka district as the established expatriate residential zone, and comprehensive staff vetting adapted to the Malagasy market. Mining sector companies, development finance organisations, and NGOs with Antananarivo residential programmes operate against a medium-risk security backdrop of violent street crime, carjacking, and periodic political instability. Source: FCDO Madagascar travel advice (2026); US State Dept Madagascar Level 2 advisory (2026).

For the full Antananarivo security picture, see our Antananarivo city briefing. Clients requiring personal close protection alongside residential security can review executive protection in Antananarivo.

item.title: Residential Security in Chisinau, Moldova
item.description:
Residential security in Chisinau addresses the specific requirements of expatriate executives, diplomatic staff, and high-net-worth individuals requiring protected accommodation in Moldovan Transnistria and Russian influence environment. Fcdo advises a high degree of caution; transnistria against all travel; us state dept level 2 (2026).

A professional residential security deployment for Chisinau begins with a property security survey covering perimeter, access control, natural surveillance, lighting, and neighbourhood risk. Guard-force selection follows, with all personnel vetted to the standards of the Moldovan Ministry of Internal Affairs. Emergency response planning – covering medical emergency (nearest facility Spitalul Clinic Republican, medevac to Bucharest), fire, intrusion, and civil disturbance – is completed before occupancy begins.

item.description:

A professional residential security deployment for Cotonou begins with a property security survey covering perimeter, access control, natural surveillance, lighting, and neighbourhood risk. item.title: Residential Security in Freetown
item.description: Residential security in Freetown operates in a high-risk environment requiring comprehensive compound hardening, Private Security Industry Act-licensed guard teams, Aberdeen peninsula or Hill Station as the recommended residential zones, and a West Africa medevac capability pre-positioned before any programme commences. Post-election political unrest adds political monitoring and evacuation planning to the standard residential security requirements. Source: FCDO Sierra Leone travel advice (2026); US State Dept Sierra Leone Level 2 advisory (2026).

item.title: Residential Security in Gaborone
item.description:
Residential security in Gaborone operates within Botswana’s low-risk, well-regulated environment. Diamond sector expatriates, development finance professionals, and diplomatic staff benefit from Private Security Industry Regulation Act-licensed operators, proportionate compound hardening, and domestic staff vetting that takes advantage of Botswana’s functional civil registry and police clearance system. Source: FCDO Botswana travel advice (2026).

For the full Gaborone security context, see our Gaborone city briefing. Clients requiring personal EP alongside their residential programme can review executive protection in Gaborone.

item.title: Residential Security in Maseru
item.description:
Residential security in Maseru is structured around LMPS-licensed operators, the Maseru Central diplomatic area as the recommended residential zone, and medium-risk hardening specifications that address Lesotho’s documented armed robbery and vehicle crime environment. Diamond sector and development finance expatriates benefit from South Africa’s proximity for both emergency evacuation and medical medevac routing. Source: FCDO Lesotho travel advice (2026).

For the full Maseru security context, see our Maseru city briefing. Clients requiring personal EP alongside their residential programme can review executive protection in Maseru.

item.title: Residential Security in Mbabane
item.description:
Residential security in Mbabane operates within Eswatini’s low-risk, small-market operating environment. Agribusiness, manufacturing, and diplomatic residential programmes benefit from licensed operators with Eswatini-specific credentials, the Ezulwini Valley as the primary long-term residential zone, and medevac planning via South Africa that takes advantage of the country’s proximity to Nelspruit and Johannesburg. Source: FCDO Eswatini travel advice (2026).

For the full Mbabane security context, see our Mbabane city briefing. Clients requiring personal EP alongside their residential programme can review executive protection in Mbabane.

item.title: Residential Security in Monrovia
item.description: Residential security in Monrovia operates in a high-risk post-conflict environment requiring comprehensive compound hardening, LNP-licensed guard teams, Mamba Point or Sinkor as the recommended residential zones, and West Africa medevac capability pre-positioned before any programme commences. The post-conflict institutional fragility that limits police response capacity drives a higher compound hardening and guard staffing standard than in comparable-risk capitals. Source: FCDO Liberia travel advice (2026); US State Dept Liberia Level 2 advisory (2026).

item.title: Residential Security in Ouagadougou
item.description: Residential security in Ouagadougou is not a viable programme under the current extreme risk conditions. The FCDO advises against all travel to Burkina Faso, including Ouagadougou, and the US State Department classifies the country at Level 4 (Do Not Travel). This page serves organisations monitoring the situation, managing wind-down of prior programmes, or planning remote management from Abidjan or Accra. Source: FCDO Burkina Faso travel advice (2026); US State Dept Burkina Faso Level 4 advisory (2026).

item.description:

Financial sector expatriates, family office principals, and HNWI residents benefit from PSIA-licensed residential guard teams, proportionate access management, and programme elements calibrated for a low-threat, high-privacy operating environment. Cyclone season planning is the most materially distinct element of Mauritius residential programmes compared with continental African deployments. Source: FCDO Mauritius travel advice (2026).

For the full Port Louis security context, see our Port Louis city briefing. Clients requiring personal security alongside their residential programme can review executive protection in Port Louis.

item.description:
Residential security in Victoria, Seychelles is calibrated for the low-risk Indian Ocean island environment, focusing on personal privacy, access management, domestic staff vetting, and outer island transfer security for HNWI and financial sector residents. The compact Mahe geography and the island’s international community create a distinct operating context from continental African residential programmes. Source: FCDO Seychelles travel advice (2026).

For the full Victoria security context, see our Victoria, Seychelles city briefing. Clients requiring personal EP alongside their residential programme can review executive protection in Victoria.

item.description:

Mining sector expatriates, corporate residential staff, and diplomatic personnel benefit from Security Officers’ Act-licensed operators, Klein Windhoek as the established residential zone, and vehicle security planning that addresses the specific break-in risk in commercial areas. Source: FCDO Namibia travel advice (2026).

For the full Windhoek security context, see our Windhoek city briefing. Clients requiring personal EP alongside their residential programme can review executive protection in Windhoek.

A professional residential security deployment for Yerevan begins with a property security survey covering perimeter, access control, natural surveillance, lighting, and neighbourhood risk. Emergency response planning – covering medical emergency (nearest facility Nairi Medical Centre, medevac to Tbilisi or Istanbul), fire, intrusion, and civil disturbance – is completed before occupancy begins.

item.title: Sarajevo
item.description: Sarajevo is the capital of Bosnia and Herzegovina, a country whose contemporary security environment is fundamentally shaped by the 1992-1995 war – Europe’s deadliest conflict since the Second World War – and the complex governance structure established by the 1995 Dayton Agreement to end it. The city itself carries a powerful historical weight: it was the site of a three-and-a-half year siege (April 1992-February 1996) by Bosnian Serb forces and Bosniak and Croat community division that has only partially healed in the three decades since.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Antananarivo
item.description:
Ivato International Airport (TNR) is 14km from central Antananarivo via the Autoroute d’Ivato. The transfer passes through suburban commercial and residential areas where vehicle-targeting incidents have been recorded after dark. FCDO Madagascar travel advice (2026) advises a high degree of caution, and the US State Department rates Madagascar at Level 2 (Exercise Increased Caution).

The TNR arrival environment

Antananarivo serves international flights from Nairobi, Addis Ababa, Paris, Reunion, Mauritius, and Johannesburg. The airport’s professional client base spans the mining sector (Ambatovy, Rio Tinto QMM), NGO and humanitarian organisations, development finance institutions, and vanilla and agribusiness sector visitors. Night arrivals on the 14km Autoroute corridor require enhanced transfer protocols.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Freetown
item.description:
Lungi International Airport (FNA) is separated from Freetown by the Sierra Leone River estuary. The absence of a road bridge means that the Lungi crossing – helicopter, ferry, or overland – is the defining operational planning task for every Freetown airport transfer. FCDO Sierra Leone travel advice (2026) advises a high degree of caution, and the US State Department rates Sierra Leone at Level 2.

The FNA arrival environment

Freetown serves arrivals from Brussels, London, Casablanca, Accra, Abidjan, Dakar, and Nairobi. The professional client base spans the NGO and humanitarian sector (UNHCR, WFP, MSF, Save the Children), the diamond and rutile mining sector, development finance organisations, and bilateral government missions. Night arrivals require helicopter crossing or enhanced ferry protocols.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Gaborone
item.description:
Sir Seretse Khama International Airport (GBE) is approximately 15km north of central Gaborone. The transfer to the CBD, Fairgrounds financial district, and Diamond Technology Park is straightforward and low-risk. FCDO travel advice rates Botswana as requiring normal travel precautions, and the US State Department rates Botswana at Level 1.

The GBE arrival environment

Gaborone serves international arrivals from Johannesburg, Nairobi, Addis Ababa, and London. The professional client base spans the diamond sector (Debswana, rough diamond trading), SADC institutional visitors, development finance organisations, and regional corporate executives. Cross-border road arrivals from Johannesburg via Tlokweng Gate are a common alternative to direct GBE arrivals.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Maseru
item.description:
Maseru’s two primary entry points – Moshoeshoe I International Airport (MSU) and the Maseru Bridge road crossing from South Africa – both require pre-arranged secure transfer arrangements. FCDO advises a heightened degree of caution in Lesotho, and the US State Department rates Lesotho at Level 2.

The Maseru arrival environment

Maseru serves the diamond sector (Gem Diamonds Letseng), development finance organisations (World Bank, AfDB, LHWP bilateral donors), NGO and humanitarian missions, and textile sector business visitors. The dual entry point structure – air via MSU or road via Maseru Bridge from Johannesburg – means that transfer planning must cover both options and cannot default to airport-only protocols.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Mbabane
item.description:
Eswatini’s dual airport structure – King Mswati III International Airport (MTS) at 80km from Mbabane and Matsapha Airport (SHO) at 25km – creates a transfer planning decision that is confirmed at the pre-deployment stage. Both airports require pre-arranged secure transfer arrangements with REPS-authorised drivers and operations controller oversight.

The Mbabane arrival environment

Mbabane serves business visitors to Eswatini’s sugar, soft drink concentrate, and timber industries, alongside diplomatic and NGO missions and a limited mining sector. The Mbabane-Manzini corridor is the primary business movement zone; most professional visits require multiple transfers between the two cities during the itinerary.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Monrovia
item.description: Roberts International Airport (ROB) is approximately 60km southeast of Monrovia – one of the longest airport-to-capital transfers in West Africa and among the longest on the continent. The extended corridor, documented vehicle-targeting risk on the highway, and the high-risk overall security environment in Liberia make pre-arranged secure transfer from ROB an operational requirement, not an optional service. FCDO Liberia travel advice (2026) advises a high degree of caution, and the US State Department rates Liberia at Level 2.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Ouagadougou
item.description:
Ouagadougou Airport (OUA) is approximately 5km from the city centre. The compact airport-to-city distance does not reduce the security requirement in an environment where FCDO advises against all travel and the US State Department rates Burkina Faso at Level 4. JNIM and IS Sahel Province have conducted attacks in Ouagadougou itself; transfer requires specialist Sahel operators, not standard corporate transport.

The OUA arrival environment

Ouagadougou serves essential operations arrivals: UN agency staff, diplomatic missions, mining companies with pre-existing infrastructure, and journalists operating in the Sahel. The junta’s expulsion of French forces and engagement with Africa Corps has fundamentally altered the operational security architecture. All arrivals require host-nation coordination and confirmed extraction planning before landing.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Port Louis
item.description:
Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam International Airport (MRU) is approximately 50km southeast of Port Louis via the motorway. At 50km, this is among the longer airport-to-capital transfers in the Indian Ocean island region, creating an extended journey that requires confirmed vehicle positioning and operations controller oversight even in Mauritius’s stable, low-crime environment.

The MRU arrival environment

Port Louis’s international arrivals at MRU include financial sector visitors from Dubai, Singapore, Hong Kong, and London connecting to Mauritius’s offshore financial centre, HNWI and private banking clients, and corporate executives. The professional client base benefits from discreet, low-profile transfer arrangements from MRU that match Mauritius’s business-friendly, upmarket environment.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Victoria, Seychelles
item.description:
Seychelles International Airport (SEZ) is approximately 10km from Victoria on Mahe Island via the coast road. The transfer is compact and low-risk by regional standards; the primary planning complexity for Seychelles transfers is inter-island logistics – connecting HNWI principals from SEZ to Praslin, La Digue, or outer island estates – rather than road crime risk.

The SEZ arrival environment

Seychelles arrivals at SEZ include HNWI principals on private charters, financial sector visitors to Victoria’s International Business Authority, and resort guests connecting to Mahe and outer island properties. The discreet, low-profile environment expected by HNWI visitors is reflected throughout the transfer protocol.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Windhoek
item.description:
Hosea Kutako International Airport (WDH) is approximately 42km east of central Windhoek via the B6 highway. At 42km, this is one of the longer airport-to-city transfers in southern Africa, requiring confirmed departure timing and active corridor management on both the inbound and departure legs. FCDO rates Namibia at normal travel precautions; the primary operational planning consideration at WDH is the transfer distance rather than crime risk.

The WDH arrival environment

Windhoek serves international arrivals from Johannesburg, Frankfurt, Cape Town, and regional connections. The professional client base spans the mining sector (Namdeb, Rossing, Husab), development finance organisations, diplomatic arrivals, and conservation and tourism sector visitors. Domestic connections from WDH to Eros Airport for mine-site flights are a common second leg for mining sector visitors.

item.title: Security Drivers in Chisinau, Moldova
item.description:
Security driver services in Chisinau address the transport security requirements of corporate and executive principals operating in Moldovan Transnistria and Russian influence environment. Fcdo advises a high degree of caution; transnistria against all travel; us state dept level 2 (2026).

All vetted security drivers for Chisinau operations must demonstrate: current valid Moldova commercial driving licence, criminal record clearance, professional references, and demonstrated knowledge of Chisinau’s road network and security geography. Airport transfers from Chisinau International Airport (KIV) use pre-arranged vehicles positioned before the principal exits customs. No app-based or informal transport is appropriate for principal movements in this environment.
