item.title: Residential Security in Bangalore
item.description: Bangalore is the centre of India’s technology economy and hosts a large and growing expatriate residential community of technology sector professionals, senior executives, and investment community personnel across Whitefield, Electronic City, Koramangala, and Indiranagar. FCDO rates Bangalore at low to moderate risk. The residential security requirement is proportionate to an environment where expat properties are visible targets for residential burglary, domestic staff vetting is important given the domestic labour market dynamics, and the Cauvery protest risk creates periodic access disruption that benefits from advance planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Beirut
item.description:
Beirut residential security operates at the critical-risk end of the spectrum, with specific requirements not present in most other global residential security programmes: power backup security system design, post-2020 structural vulnerability assessment, politically-aware domestic staff vetting, and a documented residential evacuation plan. Ministry of Interior-licensed operators are the regulatory foundation.

For the full Beirut threat picture and FCDO Lebanon advisory, see our Beirut city page. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their Beirut residential programme, executive protection in Beirut covers the full Ministry of Interior-licensed personal security detail.

item.title: Residential Security in Casablanca
item.description: Casablanca is North Africa’s primary commercial centre and hosts a significant corporate and financial sector expat community across the Maarif, Gauthier, and Ain Diab residential districts. The city’s overall risk rating is low. The residential security programme addresses the specific concerns relevant to expat households: property burglary in known expat areas, domestic staff vetting in a market where verification requires structure, and legal compliance management for a jurisdiction with strict laws that affect daily household operation.

item.title: Residential Security in Colombo
item.description:
Colombo residential security serves a medium-risk city with a post-2019 terrorism-aware property assessment framework, NSC-licensed guard deployment, and standard domestic staff vetting protocols. The residential programme is substantially less complex than Beirut or Dhaka but requires specific attention to the post-Easter-bombing threat geography and the Sri Lanka medical evacuation routing.

For the full Colombo security context, see our Colombo city page. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their Colombo residential programme, executive protection in Colombo covers the full NSC-licensed personal security detail.

item.title: Residential Security in Dar es Salaam
item.description:
Dar es Salaam residential security serves a high-risk environment with a significantly elevated perimeter and access control specification relative to peer African cities. PSRA-licensed guard deployment, specific night patrol protocols, insider-information-aware domestic staff vetting, and a documented medical evacuation protocol are the foundations of the Tanzania residential programme.

For the full Dar es Salaam security picture, see our Dar es Salaam city page. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their residential security programme in Tanzania, executive protection in Dar es Salaam covers the full PSRA-licensed close protection detail.

item.title: Residential Security in Delhi
item.description:
Delhi is India’s capital and a major destination for diplomatic missions, government-sector business, and senior corporate visits. The FCDO rates Delhi at moderate risk. Residential security for corporate assignees and expats in the capital needs to address a genuine terrorism background, a documented sexual assault risk for female principals, and a domestic staff market that requires systematic vetting to manage the fraud and disclosure risk.

PSARA-licensed Delhi state operators deliver all residential guard deployments and surveys. The Delhi city briefing provides current FCDO context, including the terrorism assessment and district-level risk notes. For principals requiring mobile close protection alongside their residential security programme, executive protection in Delhi covers the full personal security detail and CP officer deployment across the capital.

item.title: Residential Security in Dhaka
item.description:
Dhaka residential security addresses a high-risk capital with specific requirements for terrorism-adjacent venue awareness in the Gulshan zone, hartal day compound lockdown planning, apartment building access control, and enhanced domestic staff vetting for migrant household workers. Ministry of Home Affairs-registered operators are the regulatory foundation.

For the full Dhaka security picture, see our Dhaka city page. Principals requiring executive protection alongside their Dhaka residential programme should review executive protection in Dhaka for the full Ministry-licensed close protection programme.

item.title: Residential Security in Geneva
item.description: Geneva is Switzerland’s international city and one of the world’s most significant HNWI residential locations, combining private banking wealth with a diplomatic community serving the UN, WTO, WHO, and hundreds of other international organisations. The city’s ambient crime statistics are excellent. The residential security requirement for Geneva’s HNWI and diplomatic households is driven by wealth-targeting risk, the intelligence collection environment around multilateral processes, and the operational standards expected in one of the world’s most discreet and professionally managed residential security markets.

item.title: Residential Security in Ho Chi Minh City
item.description:
Ho Chi Minh City residential security serves a low-risk city with specific considerations for condominium building security infrastructure, motorbike theft prevention, and Vietnam’s Ministry of Public Security licensing constraint for all guard deployments. The residential programme is substantially less complex than Dhaka or Dar es Salaam but requires MPS-licensed operators and structured domestic staff vetting.

For the broader Ho Chi Minh City security context, see our Ho Chi Minh City page. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their residential security programme in Vietnam, executive protection in Ho Chi Minh City covers the full MPS-licensed close protection detail.

item.title: Residential Security in Kampala
item.description:
Kampala residential security serves a high-risk East African capital with specific compound perimeter standards, night guard patrol protocols, LC1-based domestic staff vetting, and boda boda-aware compound entry management. UPF-licensed operators under the Private Security Organisations Act are the regulatory foundation.

For the full Kampala security picture including the ADF terrorism context, see our Kampala city page. Principals requiring executive protection alongside their Kampala residential programme should review executive protection in Kampala for the full UPF-licensed close protection detail.

item.title: Residential Security in Kyiv
item.description:
Kyiv residential assignments exist to support diplomatic and essential personnel who are required to maintain a presence in Ukraine’s capital during the ongoing conflict. The FCDO advises against all travel to Ukraine. The information here is for those who have determined that the assignment is unavoidable and need to reduce the risks that the environment presents.

Generator-powered accommodation with confirmed basement shelter access is the minimum property standard. Wartime-experienced operators with current Ukraine knowledge deliver all guard deployments. Every Kyiv residential assignment includes a mandatory evacuation plan built around overland transit to Poland before the assignment begins.

item.title: Residential Security in Mombasa
item.description:
Mombasa residential security serves a high-risk coastal environment with specific considerations for beach and marine perimeter management not present in inland city residential programmes. PSRA-licensed operators under Kenya’s Security Industry Act 2018 are the regulatory foundation; DCI-verified domestic staff vetting and specific night guard coastal patrol protocols complete the programme.

For the full Mombasa security context including al-Shabaab awareness, see our Mombasa city page. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their Mombasa coastal residential programme, executive protection in Mombasa covers the full PSRA-licensed personal security detail.

item.title: Residential Security in Montreal
item.description:
Montreal is one of Canada’s principal commercial cities with a significant HNWI residential community across Westmount, Outremont, and Mont-Royal. Canada’s overall stability means the residential security environment is low risk. The specific concerns for Montreal residential assignments are HNWI wealth-targeting risk, the foreign state interference environment documented by CSIS, and the operational requirements of running residential security through a demanding Montreal winter.

BSPCE-licensed operators with bilingual French-English capability deliver all Montreal residential guard deployments and surveys. Winter cold-weather protocols are a standard element of all deployments running between November and March. The Montreal city briefing provides current CSIS foreign interference advisory context and the BSPCE regulatory framework. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their residential programme, executive protection in Montreal covers the HNWI event security and bilingual CP officer capability across the city.

item.title: Residential Security in Muscat
item.description:
Muscat residential security serves one of the Gulf’s lowest-risk residential environments. The primary considerations are ROP-licensed guard deployment, Omani domestic worker vetting through the TAQDEER platform and ROP clearance, and Al Mouj marina property-specific perimeter management for HNWI residents. The overall security specification is lower than Gulf peers with higher crime profiles.

For the broader Muscat and Oman security context, see our Muscat city page. For HNWI principals requiring executive protection alongside their Muscat residential programme, executive protection in Muscat covers the full ROP-licensed personal security detail.

item.title: Residential Security in Naples
item.description: Naples is one of Italy’s most distinctive cities and the gateway to one of the world’s most desirable coastal regions, with HNWI demand for residential properties in Chiaia, Posillipo, Vomero, and the Amalfi coastal villages. The residential security programme for Naples addresses the specific concerns that distinguish it from northern Italian cities: the Camorra’s influence in local commercial and labour markets requiring more thorough contractor and staff vetting, scooter-borne theft and vehicle security, and the extension of the residential programme to coastal Campania where different access and emergency protocols apply.

item.title: Residential Security in Rome
item.description: Rome is Italy’s capital and the location of the Vatican, hosting a significant diplomatic and HNWI residential community across Parioli, EUR, and the Aventino hill. The city’s ambient security environment is manageable. The residential security programme for diplomatic and HNWI households addresses the specific concerns relevant to these profiles: residential burglary and vehicle targeting in the Parioli area, organised pickpocket awareness for household staff and guests, and ZTL zone vehicle management for properties with restricted access considerations.

item.title: Residential Security in Shanghai
item.description: Shanghai is China’s commercial capital and hosts one of the world’s largest expat residential communities of technology, finance, and professional services assignees in the Jing’an, French Concession, and Pudong areas. The physical crime profile in expat residential areas is low. The residential security programme for Shanghai assignees focuses on domestic staff vetting, digital security in an environment where sensitive commercial activity operates within a state-monitored infrastructure, and legal risk briefing covering the exit ban framework that can affect assignees with significant commercial activity in China.

item.title: Residential Security in Tashkent
item.description:
Tashkent residential security addresses a medium-risk capital with specific requirements not typical of most cities: SNB-aware guard personnel vetting to protect household confidentiality, Soviet-era building lobby security upgrades, and a communications security component reflecting the active intelligence monitoring environment. MVD-licensed operators are the regulatory foundation.

For the broader Tashkent and Uzbekistan security context, see our Tashkent city page. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their Tashkent residential programme, executive protection in Tashkent covers the full MVD-licensed close protection detail.

item.title: Residential Security in Tbilisi
item.description:
Tbilisi residential security serves a medium-risk city with specific considerations for historic building access control in the Old Town, political protest proximity in the central districts, and the Georgian Ministry of Internal Affairs licensing framework. The overall security specification is lower than high-risk East African or South Asian cities but requires specific attention to Tbilisi’s distinct residential property stock and political context.

For the full Tbilisi security picture, see our Tbilisi city page. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their Tbilisi residential programme, executive protection in Tbilisi covers the full Ministry-licensed personal security detail across Georgia.

item.title: Residential Security in Warsaw
item.description:
Warsaw is Poland’s capital and has become the primary NATO support hub for Ukraine-related diplomatic, defence, and reconstruction activity. The city’s ambient residential security environment is low risk by European standards. The specific elevated concern for Warsaw residential assignments is the intelligence environment: Poland’s ABW has documented active Russian and Belarusian intelligence operations targeting individuals with NATO-adjacent and Ukraine-support roles, including through approaches to their domestic and personal contacts.

Polish Physical Security Services Act-licensed operators deliver all residential guard deployments and surveys. The counter-intelligence component of Warsaw residential programmes addresses the specific threat pattern documented by the ABW rather than generic European capital precautions. The Warsaw city briefing provides current ABW context and regional security assessment. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their residential security programme, executive protection in Warsaw covers the full counter-intelligence aware security detail for the city.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Abidjan
item.description:
Felix Houphouet-Boigny International Airport sits at the edge of Abidjan’s most challenging transit zone. The route from ABJ to the principal business districts of Le Plateau, Cocody, and Zone 4 crosses communes that FCDO and US OSAC reporting identify as carrying the highest vehicle crime rates in Abidjan. Unvetted airport taxis operating from the ABJ kerb are a documented vector for robbery of foreign arrivals.

The ABJ arrival risk window

The period between clearing baggage claim and reaching a secure vehicle is the most exposed moment in any Abidjan itinerary. Inside-terminal collection by a Ministry of Interior-licensed driver eliminates the kerb exposure. An operations controller tracking the route from collection to destination addresses the transit risk through Yopougon and at the Ebrié Lagoon bridge chokepoints.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Abuja
item.description:
Nnamdi Azikiwe International Airport is 45 kilometres from central Abuja along a corridor that FCDO and Control Risks assessments identify as carrying genuine kidnap-for-ransom risk. The extended transfer distance, combined with Nigeria’s documented targeting of foreign nationals in the FCT, makes the ABV transfer the highest-risk element of most corporate visits to Abuja. Unvetted airport taxis have been linked to robbery and express crime.

The ABV transfer risk

Inside-terminal collection eliminates kerb exposure. NSCDC-licensed drivers, kidnap-aware route planning, and operations controller monitoring throughout address the primary vulnerabilities on the Nnamdi Azikiwe Expressway corridor. Nighttime arrivals apply an enhanced protocol with modified route selection and increased check-in frequency.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Accra
item.description: Kotoka International Airport is one of West Africa’s principal aviation hubs, and its arrivals zone is a documented location for approaches to foreign travellers. The transfer to Accra’s Airport City, Cantonments, and central business district involves routes with documented smash-and-grab risk during congested peak hours. Ghana’s Private Security Organisations Act 2020 provides a relatively strong regulatory framework for verifying operator credentials, and all transfer personnel hold current Private Security Authority registration.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Addis Ababa
item.description: Bole International Airport is 6 kilometres from Addis Ababa’s Bole Road hotel corridor, but the transfer occurs against a security backdrop that FCDO (April 2026) rates as high risk. Active regional conflicts in Tigray, Amhara, and Oromia create a security environment that affects movement within the capital. Checkpoint activity by Ethiopian security services, documented street crime against foreign nationals in the Bole zone, and the arbitrary detention risk documented by FCDO all require active management in the transfer plan.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Algiers
item.description: Houari Boumediene Airport is 20 kilometres from central Algiers, and the transfer corridor operates against a national threat background that FCDO (May 2026) rates with a high terrorism warning. While Algiers city itself has not experienced a major attack in over a decade, the sustained AQIM threat in Algeria means terrorism-aware route planning is a standard element of every transfer. Algerian Interior Ministry-registered operators and DRS-aware protocols apply to all engagements.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Almaty
item.description:
Almaty International Airport is 15 kilometres from the city’s principal business and hotel district, and the transfer operates in Kazakhstan’s intelligence-active corporate environment. FCDO and US OSAC rate Almaty’s day-to-day environment as manageable for business visitors, but the energy sector context, Kazakhstan’s post-Soviet organised crime landscape, and severe winter road conditions each require planning in any secure transfer operation.

The ALA transfer in context

Inside-terminal collection, Ministry of Internal Affairs-licensed operators, and operations controller monitoring address the primary transfer risks. Winter conditions from November through February require specific vehicle preparation and timing management on the ALA-to-city corridor.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Amman
item.description: Queen Alia International Airport is 30 kilometres from central Amman along the Desert Highway, making it one of the longer airport-to-city transfer corridors in the region. Jordan’s terrorism threat rating from FCDO is high, reflecting the country’s proximity to Syria and Iraq. Jordan’s Public Security Directorate provides effective regulation of the private security sector and a strong domestic counter-terrorism record, but pre-arranged, vetted transfers remain the appropriate baseline for business arrivals.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Ankara
item.description:
Esenboga International Airport is 28 kilometres from central Ankara, and the transfer corridor operates against a threat background that FCDO rates as very likely to involve terrorism activity across Turkey. Ankara has experienced significant politically motivated attacks. Turkey’s Private Security Law No. 5188 provides a structured licensing framework, and transfer operators hold current Security General Directorate credentials.

The Esenboga transfer in context

Inside-terminal collection, Security General Directorate-licensed operators, terrorism-aware route planning on the Esenboga Highway, and operations controller monitoring throughout address the primary elements of the Ankara transfer risk picture.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Baghdad
item.description:
Baghdad International Airport transfers are armoured vehicle operations as standard. FCDO advises against all but essential travel to Iraq, and the residual IED, militia, and checkpoint risks on the BIAP-to-city corridor require planning well beyond that applicable to most other international destinations. Iraq’s Interior Ministry PMC licensing framework, established after the 2007 Nisoor Square incident, provides the regulatory basis for operator verification.

The BIAP transfer in the Baghdad threat environment

Armoured collection at BIAP, Interior Ministry PMC-registered operators, IED-aware route planning, checkpoint navigation protocols, and an operations controller with US and British Embassy emergency contacts address the primary transfer risk elements in Baghdad.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Baku
item.description: Heydar Aliyev International Airport is 20 kilometres from Baku’s principal hotel and business district. The transfer operates in Azerbaijan’s energy sector intelligence environment, where principals associated with AIOC and BTC pipeline activity arrive on known schedules that can attract state and commercial intelligence interest. MVN licensing under Azerbaijan’s Law on Private Protective Activities provides the regulatory basis for operator verification, and the FCDO does not advise against travel to Baku.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Bangalore
item.description:
Kempegowda International Airport’s position 40 kilometres from Bangalore’s IT corridor creates a transfer environment shaped by some of India’s most congested road conditions. PSARA-licensed Karnataka drivers, operations controller monitoring, and Cauvery protest route contingency planning are the foundations of the Bangalore secure transfer service.

For the broader Bangalore security picture, see the Bangalore city briefing for the current FCDO advisory and Cauvery protest pattern. For principals requiring a dedicated close protection officer alongside their airport transfer, close protection officers in Bangalore covers the CPO deployment and assignment briefing for Karnataka.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Casablanca
item.description:
Mohammed V International Airport’s position 30 kilometres from Casablanca’s CFC and Maarif business districts, combined with the documented activity by unofficial transport operators in the arrivals area, makes a pre-arranged inside-terminal collection the appropriate standard for all business arrivals. Ministry of Interior Law 27-06 licensed operators, a pre-transfer Morocco legal briefing, and operations controller monitoring are the foundations of the Casablanca secure transfer service.

For the full Casablanca security picture, see the Casablanca city briefing for the FCDO advisory, the legal environment notes, and district-level risk assessment. For principals requiring a dedicated CPO alongside their airport transfer, close protection officers in Casablanca covers the Ministry of Interior-licensed CPO deployment.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Delhi
item.description:
Indira Gandhi International Airport’s T3 processes most international traffic to India’s capital. PSARA-licensed Delhi state operators, terrorism-aware transfer protocols calibrated to the FCDO’s high-alert period calendar, and specific female principal safety measures address the documented threat environment for the capital.

For the broader Delhi security picture, see the Delhi city briefing for the full FCDO advisory and district-level assessment. For principals requiring a dedicated close protection officer alongside their airport transfer, close protection officers in Delhi covers the PSARA-licensed CPO deployment and multi-operator detail capability.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Geneva
item.description:
Geneva International Airport’s combination of diplomatic traffic and private banking arrivals creates a specific transfer requirement that goes beyond standard European hub service. Cantonal-licensed operators, espionage-aware collection protocols for multilateral principals, and discreet HNWI transfer standards are the foundations of the Geneva secure transfer service.

For the full Geneva security context, see the Geneva city briefing for the espionage environment assessment and the cantonal licensing framework. For principals requiring a dedicated CPO alongside their Geneva transfer, close protection officers in Geneva covers the cantonal-licensed CPO deployment for UN, WTO, and private banking assignments.

item.title: Secure Airport Transfers in Kyiv
item.description:
Kyiv ground transfer services exist for essential-visit principals who have assessed that travel to Ukraine is necessary and have accepted the risk that the FCDO advises against. Ukrainian airspace is closed. All entry and departure is overland. item.description: This page is about the people who provide close protection in Zurich: who they are, how they are vetted, and what credentials to verify before they deploy. item.title: Event Security in Abidjan
item.description: Abidjan is West Africa’s principal commercial city and a recurring host for ECOWAS summits and large international conferences at Hotel Ivoire and Sofitel Abidjan. The FCDO advises heightened awareness of terrorism risk in Ivory Coast, linked to instability in the Sahel region to the north, and this assessment shapes every event security plan we develop for the city. Private security operators in Abidjan are licensed by the Ministere de l’Interieur, and we engage only verified, licensed local partners for armed response and close protection deployments. For delegates planning to attend conferences in Abidjan, our Abidjan city security overview sets out the broader threat environment and transport considerations.

item.title: Event Security in Abuja
item.description: Abuja serves as Nigeria’s federal capital and a recurring host city for ECOWAS, African Union and government-adjacent conferences, most commonly at the Transcorp Hilton Abuja, which is the FCT’s primary international conference property. The FCDO assesses Nigeria as a high-risk destination, citing terrorism, kidnapping and civil unrest as active threats that apply across the country, including in the FCT. Private security companies operating in Nigeria must be licensed by the Nigeria Security and Civil Defence Corps (NSCDC) under the NSCDC Act 2003, and we engage only registered, licensed local partners for all armed response and guarding roles. For delegates and event organisers, our Abuja city security overview provides a fuller picture of the FCT operating environment.

item.title: Event Security in Accra
item.description: Accra has emerged as a preferred host city for international conferences and ECOWAS-related meetings, partly because Ghana’s comparatively stable political environment offers a more predictable operating context than many regional alternatives. The Accra International Conference Centre, Kempinski Hotel Gold Coast City and Labadi Beach Hotel are the primary properties for summit-level events. Ghana’s Private Security Organisations Act 2020 establishes the licensing framework for private security companies operating in the country, and we engage only Act-compliant operators for guarding, crowd management and close protection roles in Accra. For a fuller picture of the security environment, see our Accra city security overview.

item.title: Event Security in Addis Ababa
item.description: Addis Ababa is Africa’s diplomatic capital, hosting the headquarters of the African Union and serving as a regular venue for AU summits, IGAD meetings and World Health Assembly for Africa sessions at the UN Conference Centre, Sheraton Addis and Hilton Addis. Ethiopia’s Ministry of Trade and Industry licences private security companies operating in the country, and we engage only registered, licensed local partners for all operational roles. The FCDO rates Ethiopia as a high-risk destination due to regional conflict and instability, and this assessment directly shapes the planning framework we apply to every event brief in the city. For the broader operating environment, our Addis Ababa city security overview provides relevant context on transport, threat categories and emergency contacts.

item.title: Event Security in Algiers
item.description: Algiers is a working venue for government-adjacent trade events and international business meetings, most notably the Foire Internationale d’Alger at the Palais des Expositions, which draws exhibitors and delegations from across North Africa and the wider Mediterranean region. Algeria’s Ministry of Interior licences private security companies operating in the country, and we engage only registered, licensed local operators for all guarding and close protection roles. The FCDO advises a high degree of vigilance in Algeria, citing residual terrorism risk, and this advisory shapes the threat-aware planning framework we apply to every Algiers event brief. For a fuller picture of the operating environment, see our Algiers city security overview.

item.title: Event Security in Almaty
item.description: Almaty is Kazakhstan’s largest city and its primary commercial and conference centre, hosting SCO-related meetings, international business events and diplomatic gatherings at venues including Almaty Arena, the Almaty International Conference Hall and Rahat Palace Hotel. Kazakhstan’s Ministry of Internal Affairs (MIA) licences all private security operators, and we engage only MIA-compliant partners for every role in our Almaty deployments. The January 2022 civil unrest, which saw significant incidents in Almaty specifically, is a concrete reference point for crowd management and emergency planning: every event security brief we develop for the city incorporates enhanced protocols that reflect that experience. Our Almaty city security overview provides broader context on the security environment and transport infrastructure.

item.title: Event Security in Amman
item.description: Amman’s reputation as the Middle East’s most accessible conference city rests on Jordan’s comparatively stable political environment, its central regional location and an established international hotel and venues infrastructure. The InterContinental Amman and Four Seasons Amman are the primary properties for summit-level events, while Zara Expo handles larger exhibition formats. Jordan’s Public Security Directorate licences private security operators, and we engage only registered, licensed local partners for all roles. The FCDO advises a degree of vigilance in Jordan, noting terrorism risk heightened by proximity to regional conflicts in Syria and Iraq. This advisory is built into every event security plan we develop for Amman. For a fuller picture of the security environment, see our Amman city security overview.

item.title: Event Security in Ankara
item.description: Ankara is Turkey’s capital and the seat of the Grand National Assembly, making it a recurring host for NATO-related diplomatic meetings, intergovernmental conferences and government-adjacent business events at venues including Congresium Ankara and Bilkent Otel. Turkey’s private security sector is governed by Law No. 5188 (Ozel Guvenlik Hizmetlerine Dair Kanun), and all operators and individual personnel must be licensed under this framework. We engage only Law 5188-licensed local partners for every role in Ankara deployments. The FCDO advises heightened vigilance throughout Turkey, with specific reference to PKK-related terrorism and the risk of symbolic attacks in the capital. Counter-terrorism emergency planning is a non-negotiable element of every event brief we accept in Ankara. For broader context on the operating environment, see our Ankara city security overview.

item.title: Event Security in Auckland
item.description:
Auckland event security serves the corporate conference, private function, and HNWI event market in New Zealand’s lowest-risk major urban environment. New Zealand Private Security Personnel and Private Investigators Act 2010 CoA licensing provides the regulatory framework; SkyCity Convention Centre co-ordination and Waiheke Island maritime logistics are the operationally distinctive Auckland event security features.

New Zealand CoA regulatory compliance

Auckland event security companies hold business CoA licences; individual personnel hold crowd controller, property guard, or PPO CoA classes as appropriate. New Zealand Police Licensing and Vetting Service register confirms all status.

item.title: Event Security in Buenos Aires
item.description:
Buenos Aires event security addresses the express kidnapping threat specific to Argentina’s security environment alongside the high frequency of civil unrest that affects access routes and event vicinity risk. Ley 12.297 and CABA municipal licensing provide the regulatory framework; departure transport management is the most operationally distinctive Buenos Aires event security component.

Argentine regulatory compliance

Event security companies hold provincial Ley 12.297 or CABA municipal registration; individual guards hold credenciales habilitantes. Company registration is confirmed for the venue’s specific jurisdiction before deployment.

item.title: Event Security in Cairo
item.description:
Cairo event security operates in a context of an FCDO-assessed likely terrorism threat, extensive state surveillance, and road safety conditions that require substantive vehicle departure management at event endings. Ministry of Interior licensing through the Central Administration for Private Security provides the regulatory framework.

Egyptian Ministry of Interior regulatory compliance

Cairo event security companies hold Ministry of Interior operating permits; individual personnel hold Ministry of Interior authorisations. Company-level licensing is confirmed before deployment, with government-adjacent events requiring established Ministry of Interior co-ordination relationships.

item.title: Event Security in Jakarta
item.description:
Jakarta event security addresses an FCDO very likely terrorism threat with a documented attack history at international business hotels, extreme traffic congestion that requires active departure management, and civil unrest monitoring for demonstration activity near the Sudirman-Senayan event corridor. POLRI licensing under PP 60/2017 and Polda Metro Jaya notification requirements define the regulatory framework.

Indonesian POLRI regulatory compliance

Jakarta event security companies hold POLRI operating licences; individual security personnel hold KTA SATPAM credentials. Event notification (izin keramaian) to Polda Metro Jaya is confirmed for notifiable events.

item.title: Event Security in Manila
item.description:
Manila event security addresses a critical OSAC-rated KFR environment where event departure management, access control against KFR surveillance, and armed force posture determination are standard planning considerations specific to the Philippine security context. PNP and SOSIA licensing under RA 5487 provides the regulatory framework.

Philippine regulatory compliance

Manila event security companies hold current SOSIA company licences; individual personnel hold PNP Security Guard Licences. Armed personnel hold PNP firearm authorisations. PNP district command notification is confirmed for events above the statutory threshold.

item.title: Event Security in Melbourne
item.description: Melbourne hosts some of the Southern Hemisphere’s most prominent sporting and cultural events, drawing large international audiences to venues including the MCG, Rod Laver Arena and the Melbourne Convention and Exhibition Centre. Victoria’s Security Industry Act 1997 sets a clear licensing framework for all private security operatives, with Consumer Affairs Victoria as the licensing authority. Every crowd controller and security guard we deploy in Melbourne holds a current Consumer Affairs Victoria licence, and our event security plans are developed in coordination with Victoria Police event planning teams. For a fuller picture of Melbourne’s security environment, see our Melbourne city security overview.

item.title: Event Security in Miami
item.description:
Miami event security serves the HNWI, corporate, and entertainment sector event calendar concentrated in Art Basel, the Miami Grand Prix, and the South Florida winter season. Florida PSA Chapter 493 licensing through the Division of Licensing provides the regulatory standard; HNWI targeting at event departures and yacht-integrated maritime security are the Miami-specific event security considerations.

Florida Chapter 493 regulatory compliance

Miami event security companies hold Class D Security Agency licences; individual personnel hold Class D (and Class G if armed) licences from the Florida Division of Licensing. All licence statuses are confirmed before deployment.

item.title: Event Security in Milan
item.description: Milan is one of Europe’s principal cities for trade exhibitions, luxury events and international conferences, drawing hundreds of thousands of delegates annually to venues such as Fiera Milano and MiCo - Milan Convention Centre. Italy’s private security sector is tightly regulated under the Testo Unico delle Leggi di Pubblica Sicurezza (TULPS), which requires security companies and individual operatives to hold Prefettura-issued licences before operating in the region. Our partner network in Milan comprises only TULPS-compliant Istituti di Vigilanza Privata, and all event security plans are submitted to the Questura in accordance with Italian public event regulations. For a broader picture of the operating environment, see our Milan city security overview.

item.title: Event Security in Tel Aviv
item.description:
Tel Aviv event security operates in an active conflict environment – FCDO advises against all travel to Israel – where rocket alert shelter protocol integration, armed event security, and vehicle access control against surface attack risk are standard planning requirements rather than optional upgrades. RAAS licensing under the Ministry of Public Security provides the regulatory framework; ex-IDF and ISA-background operators provide the operational standard.

Israeli RAAS regulatory compliance

Tel Aviv event security companies hold RAAS company licences; individual personnel hold RAAS personal licences with RAAS firearms permits for armed personnel. Israel Police co-ordination is confirmed for events above the statutory notification threshold.

item.title: Event Security in Vienna
item.description:
Vienna event security operates in a unique European context: a DSN High terrorism threat post-2020, a concentration of international organisations generating significant intelligence service activity, and a calendar of diplomatic and cultural events at venues including the Hofburg, Staatsoper, and UN Vienna International Centre. GewO Section 130 and WKO licensing provide the regulatory framework; coordination with UN UNDSS and Austrian Bundespolizei Wien is standard for the city’s highest-profile events.

Austrian GewO regulatory compliance

Vienna event security companies hold Gewerbeberechtigung under GewO Section 130. Individual personnel hold WKO Grundausbildung qualifications; CPOs hold additional Personenschutz credentials. WKO register confirms company status.

item.title: Event Security in Washington DC
item.description:
Washington DC event security serves the government-adjacent, diplomatic, and corporate policy sector event calendar in a city where Congressional building access protocols, federal facility security co-ordination, and counter-intelligence awareness are standard planning components. Tri-state licensing across DC, Maryland, and Virginia provides the regulatory framework; political environment monitoring and civil disorder planning are the DC-specific event security considerations.

Tri-state regulatory compliance

DC event security companies hold DC MPDC, Maryland State Police, and Virginia DCJS licences. Individual personnel hold state licences for their deployment jurisdiction. Multi-state compliance confirmed before each deployment.

item.title: Event Security in Zurich
item.description:
Zurich event security serves the financial capital’s concentrated banking, asset management, and insurance sector event calendar, alongside cultural events at the Opera House, Kunsthaus, and Art Basel Zurich. Swiss cantonal regulation at the Kantonspolizei Zurich level, with VSBU certification for individual security personnel, provides the regulatory framework; counter-intelligence awareness and HNWI luxury targeting at event departures are the primary security considerations specific to Zurich’s event environment.

Swiss cantonal regulatory compliance

Zurich event security companies hold Kantonspolizei Zurich registration. Individual security personnel hold VSBU-certified training at the appropriate level. Both are confirmed before deployment.

item.title: Executive Protection in Abidjan
item.description: Executive protection in Abidjan is a programme, not a single officer. It is the discipline a company applies to protect a travelling executive against regional Sahel terrorism awareness and armed robbery: advance work on venues and routes, a written threat assessment, an operations controller, and secure transport integrated with the corporate itinerary. This page is for security managers designing protection around a visit, where the need is layered, documented risk management rather than ad-hoc cover.

This page is for security managers designing protection around a C-suite visit, where the need is layered, documented risk management rather than ad-hoc cover.
